label: Security Program Manager
description: Manages information security programs at the organizational level — security project portfolio management, vendor management, security budget planning, and security initiative coordination.

paths.name: Security Program Management Track
paths.rationale: CISM provides the most widely recognized security management credential. GSLC and GSTRT are GIAC security management credentials for practitioners executing programs rather than owning the full CISO function.
pros: CISM widely recognized at enterprise and government employers
GSLC and GSTRT are the most technically-grounded security management credentials
Strong combination for practitioners from technical backgrounds moving into program management
cons: GSLC and GSTRT have less market recognition than CISM at traditional HR filters

prerequisites.experience: 5+ years security experience including project or program management responsibilities.

cert_sequence.notes: ISACA CISM — the most widely recognized security management credential. Establishes the management framework recognition at enterprise and government employers before adding GIAC leadership depth.

GIAC Security Leadership Certification — security project management, communication, organizational structure, and policy. Connects technical GIAC background to management credentialing. Open-book — build indexed notes by management domain.

GIAC Strategic Planning, Policy, and Leadership — security strategy development, program alignment with business objectives, and board-level communication. Strategic capstone of the GIAC leadership track. With CISM providing recognized management credential and GSLC operational depth, GSTRT completes the strategic leadership layer.
